title: Replace Flat-Flex Cable (FFC) At The A-Axis PCA
summary: Remove the existing flat-flex cable and install the replacement FFC at the A-axis PCA using the documented lockout, access, clamp removal, cable replacement, and clamp reinstallation steps from the source manual.
when_to_use: Use this procedure when the flat-flex cable at the A-axis PCA must be replaced on the tipper, as documented in the OptiSweep Operation and Maintenance Manual.
not_for: Do not use this procedure for post-install verification or troubleshooting beyond the documented replacement steps; the source excerpt does not provide those details.
Do not use this procedure to interchange left and right ClearLink controller hardware; the source notes the hardware is the same but not interchangeable because the IP addresses are different.

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: Perform lockout/tagout, gain access as needed, remove the existing FFC, install the replacement FFC, and reinstall the clamps using the documented hardware.

safety_notes: LOTO the tipper before performing the replacement.
Preserve correct component identity where applicable because left and right ClearLink controller hardware is the same but not interchangeable due to different IP addresses.
access_or_tools_needed: Access to the station side panel and A-axis PCA

access_required: Access to the station side panel and A-axis PCA
system_preconditions: Tipper is locked out/tagged out using the referenced Operator Station Lockout/Tagout procedure.

visual_references.description: Figure showing the flat-flex cable, FFC clamps, and related clamp hardware at the A-axis PCA for replacement.
Figure identifying the ClearLink controller/controller box location for access context.
Figure identifying the Tip PCA / A-Axis PCA location for component context.

steps.title: Lock out and tag out the tipper
steps.purpose: Place the equipment in a safe state before opening panels or handling internal components.
steps.instruction: LOTO the tipper using the referenced Operator Station Lockout/Tagout procedure before starting the flat-flex cable replacement.
steps.expected_result: The tipper is locked out and tagged out in accordance with the referenced procedure.
steps.healthy_condition: The tipper is in a locked out/tagged out state before any access or replacement work begins.
steps.failure_condition: The tipper is not locked out/tagged out or the lockout state cannot be confirmed.

actions.label: Perform LOTO
actions.exact_action: LOTO the tipper using the referenced Operator Station Lockout/Tagout procedure.

stop_or_escalate_if: The tipper cannot be placed in a locked out/tagged out state.
The referenced Operator Station Lockout/Tagout procedure is unavailable.

source_evidence.text: LOTO the tipper using the referenced Operator Station Lockout/Tagout procedure.
The nearby procedure states that the tipper must be LOTO'd.

steps.title: Gather replacement part, PPE, and tools
steps.purpose: Ensure all documented materials and tools are available before opening the equipment and removing the cable.
steps.instruction: Gather the replacement flat-flex cable, safety glasses, gloves, 3/8-in. wrench or socket, 4-mm hex wrench, 6-mm hex wrench, tool to cut wire ties, and wire ties.
steps.expected_result: All documented parts, PPE, and tools are available at the work area.
steps.healthy_condition: The replacement FFC and all listed PPE and tools are present before access and removal steps begin.
steps.failure_condition: Any required replacement part, PPE, or listed tool is missing.

actions.label: Verify required items
actions.exact_action: Gather the documented replacement part, PPE, and tools: replacement flat-flex cable, safety glasses, gloves, 3/8-in. wrench or socket, 4-mm hex wrench, 6-mm hex wrench, tool to cut wire ties, and wire ties.

stop_or_escalate_if: The replacement flat-flex cable is not available.
Required PPE or listed tools are not available.

source_evidence.text: Gather the documented replacement part, PPE, and tools: replacement flat-flex cable, safety glasses, gloves, 3/8-in. wrench or socket, 4-mm hex wrench, 6-mm hex wrench, tool to cut wire ties, and wire ties.

steps.title: Remove the station side panel if needed for access
steps.purpose: Gain access to the motor and internal area needed to reach the A-axis PCA and flat-flex cable.
steps.instruction: If necessary, use a 3/8-in. wrench or socket to remove the nine self-tapping screws securing the side panel, then remove the station side panel to access the motor.
steps.expected_result: The station side panel is removed when needed and access to the internal area is available.
steps.healthy_condition: The side panel is removed only if needed, and the A-axis PCA area is accessible.
steps.failure_condition: The side panel cannot be removed as documented or access to the A-axis PCA is still obstructed.

actions.label: Remove side panel fasteners
actions.exact_action: Use a 3/8-in. wrench or socket to remove the nine self-tapping screws securing the side panel.

actions.label: Remove side panel
actions.exact_action: Remove the station side panel to access the motor.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Flat-flex cable and related clamp hardware used during FFC replacement on the tipper ClearLink controller assembly.
ClearLink controller/controller box location for access context after side panel removal.

stop_or_escalate_if: The side panel cannot be removed using the documented fasteners.
Required access to the A-axis PCA cannot be obtained after panel removal.

source_evidence.text: If necessary, use a 3/8-in. wrench or socket to remove the nine self-tapping screws securing the side panel, then remove the station side panel to access the motor.
The nearby procedure states that the station side panel may need to be removed.
The procedure instructs technicians to LOTO the tipper and remove the station side panel if needed.

steps.title: Remove the old flat-flex cable at the A-axis PCA
steps.purpose: Disconnect and remove the existing FFC from the A-axis PCA so it can be replaced.
steps.instruction: Starting at the A-axis PCA, remove the four M3 socket-head screws holding the two FFC clamps and unplug the old FFC.
steps.expected_result: The two FFC clamps are removed and the old flat-flex cable is unplugged from the A-axis PCA.
steps.healthy_condition: All four M3 socket-head screws and both FFC clamps are removed without preventing cable removal, and the old FFC is unplugged.
steps.failure_condition: The M3 socket-head screws or clamps cannot be removed, or the old FFC cannot be unplugged as documented.

actions.label: Remove FFC clamp screws
actions.exact_action: Remove the four M3 socket-head screws holding the two FFC clamps.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Flat-flex cable, FFC clamps, and M3 socket-head screw locations at the A-axis PCA.
Tip PCA / A-axis PCA location for component identification context.

stop_or_escalate_if: The four M3 socket-head screws cannot be removed.
The FFC clamps cannot be removed.
The old FFC cannot be unplugged as documented.

source_evidence.text: Starting at the A-axis PCA, remove the four M3 socket-head screws holding the two FFC clamps and unplug the old FFC.
At the A-axis PCA the technician removes four M3 socket-head screws holding two FFC clamps and unplugs the old flat-flex cable.

steps.title: Install the new flat-flex cable and reinstall clamps
steps.purpose: Complete the replacement by connecting the new FFC and restoring the clamp hardware.
steps.instruction: Plug in the new FFC and reinstall the FFC clamps using the M3 socket-head screws.
steps.expected_result: The new flat-flex cable is installed at the A-axis PCA and the FFC clamps are reinstalled with the M3 socket-head screws.
steps.healthy_condition: The new FFC is plugged in and both clamps are reinstalled using the documented screws.
steps.failure_condition: The new FFC cannot be plugged in, the clamps cannot be reinstalled, or the M3 socket-head screws cannot be reinstalled.

actions.label: Reinstall FFC clamps
actions.exact_action: Reinstall the FFC clamps using the M3 socket-head screws.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Flat-flex cable and clamp hardware placement during reinstallation at the A-axis PCA.

stop_or_escalate_if: The new FFC cannot be installed as documented.
The FFC clamps cannot be reinstalled.
The replacement cannot be completed as documented.

source_evidence.text: Plug in the new FFC and reinstall the FFC clamps using the M3 socket-head screws.
The nearby procedure states to install the new FFC and reinstall the clamps.

success_criteria: The old FFC is removed from the A-axis PCA.
The new flat-flex cable is plugged in at the A-axis PCA.
The two FFC clamps are reinstalled using the four M3 socket-head screws.
healthy_conditions: The tipper is locked out/tagged out before work begins.
Required PPE, tools, and replacement cable are available.
Access to the A-axis PCA is obtained if side panel removal is needed.
The new FFC is installed and the clamp hardware is reinstalled.
failure_conditions: LOTO cannot be completed or confirmed.
Required tools, PPE, or replacement cable are unavailable.
The side panel cannot be removed when access is needed.
The FFC clamp screws, clamps, or old cable cannot be removed as documented.
The new FFC or clamp hardware cannot be installed as documented.
Post-install verification or troubleshooting is required beyond the documented source steps.
escalation_guidance: Escalate if the replacement cannot be completed as documented.
Escalate if post-install verification or troubleshooting is needed because the source excerpt does not provide those steps.
Escalate if there is uncertainty about correct component identity, especially regarding left versus right ClearLink controller hardware.
